en.extract:
The Cochabamba mountain finch (Poospiza garleppi) is a species of bird in the family Thraupidae. It is endemic to shrubby woodland in the Andes of Bolivia. Together with the closely related Tucumán mountain finch, it is placed in the genus Poospiza. It is threatened by habitat loss.

The '''Cochabamba mountain finch''' ('''''Poospiza garleppi''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Thraupidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to shrubby woodland in the [[Andes]] of [[Bolivia]]. Together with the closely related [[Tucumán mountain finch]], it is placed in the genus ''[[Poospiza]]''.<ref>Nores, M. 2008. ''[http://www.museum.lsu.edu/~Remsen/SACCprop366.html Resurgir el Género Compsospiza.] {{webarchive |url=https://web.archive.org/web/20130411010641/http://www.museum.lsu.edu/~Remsen/SACCprop366.html |date=April 11, 2013 }}'' South American Classification Committee. Accessed 27-04-2009.</ref> It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].
